lesson: When the same HTR FAIL repeats night after night and continuity shows the mix sensor going open-circuit once hot, the sensor is the culprit. Re-running just burns another night.
symptoms: Display says HTR FAIL 12 and nothing else.
Manager says this is the third morning in a row.
Heat-treat reaches the final stage, then fails before release.
Staff have rerun it twice with the same result.
The product feels properly hot when the fault appears.
HTR FAIL returns near the end of every overnight cycle.
A manual rerun did not clear the morning lockout.
This started three nights ago and is now consistent.

tests.error-log: Heat-treat cycles repeatedly logged out of spec — the mix taking far longer than it should to register temperature.
tests.temp-probe: Your independent probe and the machine's own sensor tell two different stories — the product side measures in spec on your probe.
tests.continuity-test: Mix temp sensor reads in spec cold, then goes open-circuit as it warms past 40 °C.

flavour: The heat was real. The sensor was in denial.
authenticityNote: Sometimes the heat-treat lockout is genuine: a mix temp sensor that drops out when hot aborts every cycle. Re-running just burns another night — which is the trap, because re-running is the right call for the other HTR FAIL. The continuity test is what tells these twins apart.
